dataset.name: EID Quick Reference - VPN / Remote Access Service

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: VPN connection successfully established
entries.summary: The Remote Access Service client successfully established a VPN connection to a remote server.
entries.details: Generated in the Application event log by the RasClient provider when a VPN connection is successfully established. Key fields: ConnectionName (the VPN profile name configured on the client), RasError (0 = success), and the server address embedded in the message text. This event marks the precise moment a VPN tunnel is up and authenticated. In a DFIR context, this event is used to reconstruct a user's remote access history — correlate with Security EID 4624 (logon) and the user account to attribute VPN sessions to specific identities. Unexpected VPN connections from unusual source IPs, outside business hours, or from endpoints not previously used for VPN access warrant investigation. On a server-side RRAS deployment, pair with EID 6272/6273 in the Security log for the corresponding NPS authentication decision. The Connection Name field reflects the user-defined VPN profile — attackers who configure a new VPN profile will have a profile name not matching IT-managed standards.

investigationPivots: ConnectionName not matching IT-provisioned VPN profile naming conventions indicates a user-created or attacker-created connection profile
Correlate the connection timestamp with Security EID 4624 to identify the logon session spawned by the VPN auth
Compare the source device (Computer field) against the user's known device inventory — VPN from an unregistered device is anomalous
First-time VPN use from a user with no prior 20221 history warrants review, especially if near-simultaneous with a credential event
Correlate with Security EID 6272/6273 on the NPS/RRAS server for the auth decision and client IP
commonFalsePositives: Routine remote workers establishing their daily VPN session
Automated service accounts or monitoring systems using VPN connections
Split-tunnel VPN connections reconnecting after brief network interruptions

keyFields.name: ConnectionName
keyFields.description: VPN profile name; IT-managed profiles have standardised names — atypical names indicate user-created or attacker-configured connections

detectionRules.title: VPN Connection Outside Business Hours
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "RasClient"
| where EventID == 20221
| extend HourOfDay = datetime_part('hour', TimeGenerated)
| where HourOfDay < 7 or HourOfDay > 20
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, RenderedDescription
detectionRules.notes: Tune hours to match your organisation's expected working hours. Combine with user-to-device mapping to filter known remote workers.

detectionRules.title: VPN Connection Established via RasClient
detectionRules.rule:
title: VPN Connection Established via RasClient
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: application
detection:
    selection:
        Source: RasClient
        EventID: 20221
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - All legitimate VPN sessions from remote workers
level: informational
detectionRules.notes: Use as a baseline collector rather than a direct alert. Correlate with time-of-day, user, and device context for meaningful detection.

entries.title: VPN connection terminated
entries.summary: The Remote Access Service client disconnected from a VPN connection.
entries.details: Generated when a VPN connection is terminated, either by the user, the OS, or due to network interruption. Key fields: ConnectionName, and the disconnect reason embedded in the message. Pair with EID 20221 to calculate VPN session duration. Anomalously short sessions (seconds to a few minutes) may indicate failed lateral movement attempts or scripted connection testing. A 20222 without a preceding 20221 (e.g., if the log was cleared) can indicate log tampering. In large organisations, building a baseline of typical session durations per user allows statistical detection of outliers. Server-side, correlate with Security EID 6274 (NPS revoked access) or Security EID 4647 (logoff) on the remote host to understand the full session lifecycle.

investigationPivots: Pair with EID 20221 to calculate session duration — sessions under 60 seconds that do not correspond to a user-initiated disconnect are anomalous
Missing EID 20221 preceding a 20222 may indicate log tampering or log rotation
Correlate with Security EID 4647 on the VPN server to confirm logoff was clean vs. abrupt
commonFalsePositives: User-initiated disconnects after completing remote work
VPN disconnecting during network adapter change (e.g., switching from Wi-Fi to wired)
Idle timeout disconnects per VPN profile policy

keyFields.name: ConnectionName
keyFields.description: VPN profile name; pair with EID 20221 to bracket session duration

entries.title: VPN connection failed
entries.summary: The Remote Access Service client failed to establish a VPN connection.
entries.details: Generated when a VPN connection attempt fails. Key fields: ConnectionName and ErrorCode (the RAS error code, e.g., 691 = authentication failure, 619 = port disconnected, 789 = L2TP/IPsec pre-shared key mismatch, 868 = DNS resolution failure for server). Error 691 (authentication failure) is the most security-relevant code — repeated 20223 events with error 691 from the same device targeting the same VPN profile indicates credential brute force or password spray. A cluster of 20223 events from many different source devices targeting the same VPN infrastructure (observable on the server side) indicates a distributed credential attack. Error 789 (IPsec pre-shared key mismatch) against a site-to-site VPN may indicate an attacker probing with an incorrect key. Correlate with Security EID 6273 on the NPS server for server-side authentication rejection detail.

investigationPivots: ErrorCode 691 (authentication failure) repeated from the same device indicates credential stuffing or brute force
ErrorCode 691 across many different devices in a short window is a password spray indicator — pivot to the server-side NPS EID 6273 for the requesting IP
ErrorCode 789 (L2TP/IPsec pre-shared key failure) is unusual and may indicate misconfiguration or an attacker testing credentials
A 20223 immediately followed by a successful 20221 from the same device may indicate a retry after a transient error — verify the connection was legitimate
commonFalsePositives: User entering an incorrect VPN password — typically 1-2 failures before successful auth
Transient network failures causing DNS resolution errors (error 868)

keyFields.name: ConnectionName
keyFields.description: VPN profile name of the failed connection

keyFields.name: ErrorCode
keyFields.description: RAS error code; 691 = auth failure (credential attack), 789 = IPsec key mismatch, 868 = DNS failure

detectionRules.title: VPN Authentication Failures — Brute Force Pattern
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "RasClient"
| where EventID == 20223
| where RenderedDescription contains "691"
| summarize FailCount = count() by Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where FailCount > 5
detectionRules.notes: Error 691 is authentication failure. Threshold of 5 in 10 minutes catches automated credential testing. Adjust per environment.
